This project developed an IoT and machine learning framework for residential energy management. Sensors and an ESP32 microcontroller were used to monitor occupancy, environmental conditions, and power consumption. The system automated appliance control, reduced energy waste, improved efficiency.

This project implements a Digital Twin framework for solar panel assessment using Artificial Intelligence. It identifies micro-cracks, evaluates their severity, and estimates resulting power loss. The system analyzes solar panel images through Computer Vision and Machine Learning techniques to detect structural defects and performance degradation.

Developed a machine learning framework for predicting atmospheric Precipitable Water (PW) using GNSS-derived Zenith Total Delay (ZTD), spatial coordinates, and temporal features. TensorFlow is an open-source machine learning framework used for developing and deploying artificial intelligence and deep learning applications. It was created by Google and is now maintained by the TensorFlow open-source community.

A simple from-scratch implementation of backpropagation in Python, focusing on how gradients are computed and propagated through a computational graph. The implementation is then applied to train a basic multilayer perceptron (MLP) using gradient descent, without external machine learning frameworks.

Project is modeling and forecasting the national inflation rate of Pakistan (measured as the year-over-year percentage change in the Consumer Price Index, or CPI) using datasets spanning from 1980 to 2024, comparing a classical time-series model (ARIMA) against several machine learning frameworks (Lasso, Ridge, Elastic Net, SVR, and Random Forest).

A modular Python framework for fairness-aware post-processing in machine learning.  The library takes classifier probabilities and learns optimal decision thresholds  by optimizing multiple fairness and performance metrics, enabling multi-objective  selection via methods such as TOPSIS and Zenith.

mercury-explainability is a library with implementations of different state-of-the-art methods in the field of explainability. They are designed to work efficiently and to be easily integrated with the main Machine Learning frameworks.

A growing machine learning framework built entirely from scratch in pure Python  no external libraries such as NumPy or scikit-learn.  The goal is to deeply understand how machine learning algorithms work internally by implementing them from first principles.

In this paper, we propose a high precision bilingual machine learning framework to intercept SMS phishing in Bangladeshs MFS sector (bKash, Nagad, Rocket). FairXplain is a Trustworthy AI framework that makes machine learning decisions transparent and fair. It uses Explainable AI to show why predictions are made, audits models for hidden bias, applies fairness improvements, and generates counterfactual explanations. The system helps ensure AI decisions are understandable, accountable, and trustworthy.

Physics-informed machine learning framework for OTDR-based optical fiber fault classification.
